title: Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS)
meta_description: Senior Citizen Savings Scheme 2026 — 8.2% interest rate for retirees 60+. How to open, maximum investment ₹30 lakh, quarterly interest payout, and tax benefits under 80C.

hero.one_liner: 8.2% guaranteed interest with quarterly payouts for senior citizens — the safest and highest-return investment option for retirees

what_is_it: Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) is a government-backed savings scheme exclusively for Indian citizens aged 60 and above (55+ for retired defense personnel and 50+ for those who took VRS). It offers one of the highest guaranteed interest rates — currently 8.2% per annum — among all fixed-income investments available to senior citizens.

The maximum investment limit is ₹30 lakh (increased from ₹15 lakh in Budget 2023). The maturity period is 5 years, extendable by another 3 years. Interest is paid quarterly — directly into your bank account on April 1, July 1, October 1, and January 1 — providing regular income for retirees who need cash flow.

SCSS deposits qualify for Section 80C deduction up to ₹1.5 lakh. Additionally, senior citizens get an extra ₹50,000 deduction on interest income under Section 80TTB. Combined with the 8.2% rate, SCSS offers better post-tax returns than most alternatives for senior citizens.

The scheme is available at all post offices and authorized banks (SBI, PNB, BOB, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, etc.). It can be opened individually or jointly (with spouse). Joint account allows seamless transfer to the surviving spouse in case of the primary holder's death.

eligibility.label: Age
eligibility.value: 60 years and above (55+ for retired defense, 50+ for VRS — must invest within 1 month of retirement)

eligibility.label: Citizenship
eligibility.value: Indian citizen only. NRIs and HUFs are not eligible.

eligibility.label: Max investment
eligibility.value: ₹30 lakh per individual. Joint accounts can have ₹30 lakh each if both are seniors.

eligibility.label: Joint account
eligibility.value: Allowed only with spouse. First depositor must be the senior citizen.

eligibility.label: Multiple accounts
eligibility.value: Allowed, but total across all accounts cannot exceed ₹30 lakh

eligibility.label: Where to open
eligibility.value: Any post office or authorized bank. Carry Aadhaar, PAN, age proof, and passport-size photos.

extra_sections.heading: Quarterly Income Calculation
content: At the maximum investment of ₹30 lakh with 8.2% interest:

That's ₹20,500 per month in guaranteed income — enough to cover basic living expenses for many retired couples. And the ₹30 lakh principal is completely safe (government-backed) and returned at maturity.
Interest rate is fixed for the entire 5-year tenure at the rate prevailing on the date of investment. Even if the government reduces SCSS rates later, your rate remains locked in.

extra_sections.heading: SCSS vs FD vs PPF for Seniors

steps.title: Visit post office or bank with documents
steps.description: Carry: Aadhaar card, PAN card (mandatory for investments above ₹50,000), age proof (Aadhaar/passport/pension book), passport-size photos (2), and the cheque/DD for the investment amount.

steps.title: Fill SCSS account opening form
steps.description: Fill in your details, nominee information, and investment amount. If opening a joint account, spouse's details and photos are also needed.

steps.title: Make the deposit
steps.description: Pay by cheque (in favor of 'Postmaster' for post office, or bank branch for banks), demand draft, or transfer from your existing account at the same bank/post office. Cash deposits allowed only up to ₹1 lakh.

steps.title: Receive passbook and start getting quarterly income
steps.description: You'll receive a passbook immediately. Interest is credited to your linked savings account quarterly — first payout comes on the next interest payment date (Apr 1, Jul 1, Oct 1, or Jan 1) after completing at least one quarter.

how_to_apply.title: Visit nearest post office or authorized bank
how_to_apply.description: Carry age proof, Aadhaar, PAN, photos, and investment amount (cheque/DD).

how_to_apply.title: Fill SCSS form and make deposit
how_to_apply.description: Min ₹1,000, max ₹30 lakh. Joint account with spouse allowed.

how_to_apply.title: Quarterly interest starts
how_to_apply.description: Interest credited to bank account on Apr 1, Jul 1, Oct 1, Jan 1.

how_to_apply.title: Maturity at 5 years
how_to_apply.description: Principal returned. Option to extend for 3 more years at prevailing rate.

important_alert.text: TDS is deducted at 10% if annual interest exceeds ₹50,000 (₹40,000 for non-senior citizens in joint accounts). Submit Form 15H at your post office/bank to avoid TDS if your total income is below taxable limit.

important_dates.label: Open anytime
important_dates.value: Available throughout the year at post offices and banks

important_dates.label: Extension
important_dates.value: Can extend for 3 more years within 1 year of maturity

faqs.question: I'm 58 and took VRS. Can I open SCSS?
faqs.answer: Yes. People who have taken VRS (Voluntary Retirement Scheme) can open SCSS if they are 55 years or above. But you must invest within 1 month of receiving retirement benefits, and the investment cannot exceed the retirement benefits amount.

faqs.question: What if I need the money before 5 years?
faqs.answer: Premature closure is allowed: After 1 year but before 2 years — deduction of 1.5% of deposit. After 2 years but before 5 years — deduction of 1% of deposit. Before 1 year — not allowed. Given the penalties are small, SCSS has reasonable liquidity for seniors who may face emergency expenses.

faqs.question: Can I invest retirement corpus from EPF/gratuity in SCSS?
faqs.answer: Yes. Many retirees invest their EPF withdrawal, gratuity, and other retirement benefits in SCSS for guaranteed quarterly income. The maximum is ₹30 lakh across all SCSS accounts. This is one of the best uses of retirement lump sums.

faqs.question: Is SCSS interest fully taxable?
faqs.answer: SCSS interest is added to your income and taxed at your slab rate. However, seniors (60-80 years) have a basic exemption of ₹3 lakh under the old regime, plus ₹50,000 deduction under 80TTB specifically for interest income. For most seniors with only SCSS and savings interest income, the effective tax is zero or very low.

faqs.question: Can I have both SCSS and PPF?
faqs.answer: Yes. They serve different purposes. SCSS gives you regular quarterly income (ideal for living expenses). PPF is for long-term tax-free wealth accumulation (15 years). You can invest ₹30 lakh in SCSS + ₹1.5 lakh/year in PPF and claim 80C on both.
